nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=128 beats='{OPENING_MOMENT} leaves {HERO} shut in overnight in {THRESHOLD}, a wonderland of brass and tin and copper creatures that have all wound down and gone perfectly still; {COMPANION} rides on her shoulder, ticking softly as if to say come on, come on; four great wings open off {ENTRANCE}, {ROUTE_A_ZONE}, {ROUTE_B_ZONE}, {ROUTE_C_ZONE}, and {ROUTE_D_ZONE}, and {HERO} must pick one to explore before {DEADLINE}'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='{HERO} steps into {ROUTE_A_ZONE} where hundreds of clockwork birds sit frozen on their perches, beaks open in songs that stopped hours ago, and {COMPANION} ticks softly on her shoulder as if urging her to hurry'>>

choices.label: Walk in under the silent birds.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the aviary splits two ways, a wide floor of tall perch-trees leading deeper among the biggest birds, and a narrow spiral stair climbing to {A2_ZONE} near the dark glass roof'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='among the perch-trees {HERO} can turn toward {A1_ZONE}, or toward {A2_ZONE} where a huge brass eagle spreads its still wings'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{A1_ZONE} is a wall of tiny brass larks and wrens, each no bigger than a thumb, their little keys wound down to nothing'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{COMPANION} scampers to {A1_FIND}, and {HERO} sees it once played all the birds at once, if only it had power'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='behind {A1_FIND} {A1_CLUE} is pinned, and it speaks of {GOAL} that wakes the whole museum from {ROTUNDA}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{HERO} pockets the clue and follows {COMPANION} along {A1_PATH} that runs behind the cases toward the middle of the building'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{A1_PATH} ends at {A1_DOOR}, the heart of the museum humming faintly beyond'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{A2_ZONE} is all shadows and great still wings, a brass eagle and a bronze owl watching with glassy eyes that do not blink'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='a single long feather has fallen, {A2_FIND}, but when {HERO} lifts it she finds it is only a pretty decoy, not the real thing'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the bronze owl, though, has one wing pointing stiffly toward {A2_SIGN}, as if a keeper set it that way long ago on purpose'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{HERO} follows where the owl points, down {A2_PATH} toward the warm hum at the center of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both ways bring {HERO} to {ROUTE_A_DOOR} that opens on {ROTUNDA}, and {COMPANION} squeaks and darts ahead through the gap'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the door swings on a round hall lit by a high skylight of {DEADLINE_SIGN}, the true middle of the whole clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='in the center of the rotunda stands {CLOCK}, and {HERO} is sure {GOAL} must hang near it'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='{A2_ZONE}'s spiral stair is rickety, and halfway up {A_GATE} gives a warning crack beneath her shoe'>>

choices.label: Grab the rail and climb back down.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='{HERO} freezes, grabs the rail with both hands, and waits until the shaking stops, her heart pounding, then backs carefully down to the safe aviary floor'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='safe on the floor again, she lets out a long breath, decides {A_STUCK} is not worth the danger, and turns instead to the perch-trees'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='{HERO} climbs on despite the groaning, but {A_GATE} folds and the stair pulls away from the wall, leaving her stuck on a narrow ledge among the sleeping birds; she is quite safe, but she can only wait up there, waving to {COMPANION} below, until the museum staff arrive at opening time and fetch a ladder to bring her down'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='{ROUTE_B_ZONE} opens wide and golden, a whole plain of tin grass with {ANIMAL_LION} caught mid-roar, giraffes as tall as the ceiling, and a great elephant standing dark and heavy in the middle distance'>>

choices.label: Step out onto the tin plain.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='{HERO} can cross the open savanna floor toward the big animals, or slip along the painted horizon wall where a narrow walkway skirts the edge of the hall'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='out on the floor {HERO} can head for {B1_ZONE}, or {B2_ZONE} where the tall necks reach up into shadow'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='up close the great tin elephant of {B1_ZONE} is enormous, and as {HERO} passes, a last bit of wind makes its trunk twitch and one foot scrape, and she jumps back with a gasp'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the elephant lurches a slow half-step, groaning, not truly awake but not truly asleep, and {HERO} sees she must give it a wide and careful berth'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she ducks behind {B1_FIND} and waits until the great beast settles again with a long mechanical sigh, still at last'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='safe past the elephant, {HERO} finds {B1_PATH} in the tin grass, leading toward the heart of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{B1_PATH} ends at {B1_DOOR}, warm to the touch, with the hum of {CLOCK} behind it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{B2_ZONE} is a forest of long tin necks and gentle glass eyes, their heads lost up in the roof shadows'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='high on a shelf, almost out of reach, {HERO} spies {B2_FIND}, and a rope pulley the keeper used to raise and lower it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she works the pulley and lowers the shelf, but {B2_FIND} holds only spare oil-cans, not keys, though {B2_SIGN} points the way onward'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='following {B2_SIGN}, {HERO} crosses the grove to {B2_DOOR} humming with the warmth of the museum heart'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both routes meet at {ROUTE_B_DOOR} that opens on {ROTUNDA}, and {COMPANION} slips under it with a flick of its tail'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='beyond the arch the round rotunda waits under its {DEADLINE_SIGN}, the true middle of the clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{CLOCK} towers in the center, and {HERO} feels sure {GOAL} must hang somewhere near it'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the horizon-wall walkway is narrow, and as {HERO} edges along it {B_GATE} lurches awake below and swings its heavy trunk, groaning, right across the way ahead'>>

choices.label: Press into an alcove and wait.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='{HERO} presses flat into a shallow alcove in the wall and holds still, letting {B_GATE} sway and settle until it goes quiet, then she slips back to the open floor'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='back on the savanna floor with the elephant still again, {HERO} decides the wall walkway is too risky and picks a path among the big animals instead'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='{HERO} tries to hurry past on the narrow walkway, but {B_GATE} swings round and gently, blindly, backs her into the corner of the hall where she cannot get by; there is no harm in it, only a big slow tin beast in the way, and she must wait tucked safely in the corner until morning, when the keepers wind the elephant down and free her'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='{ROUTE_C_ZONE} is cool and blue, great copper whales hanging on wires overhead, a huge octopus coiled in a corner, and glass tanks of still copper fish glinting in the dim light'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='{HERO} can wade between the low floor tanks toward the middle of the hall, or climb the whale-rib catwalk that arches high over everything in the dark'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='among the tanks {HERO} can explore {C1_ZONE}, or {C2_ZONE} where the water-glass turns almost black'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{C1_ZONE} glitters with copper seahorses and darting fish, all frozen, and {C1_FIND} hums when {HERO} rests her hand there'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{COMPANION} taps {C1_FIND} and a panel slides back to show {C1_CLUE}, every hall drawn in careful ink'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='on {C1_CLUE} a small tag shaped like a key is tied, and a note in neat writing points to {ROTUNDA} at the museum heart'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{HERO} tucks {C1_CLUE} away and follows its inked corridors, {COMPANION} leading, toward the warm middle of the building'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the way ends at {C1_DOOR}, humming softly, {CLOCK} ticking somewhere close beyond it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{C2_ZONE} is the darkest place {HERO} has been, black glass all around, and only a faint glow far down in it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the glow comes from {C2_FIND}, casting just enough light to show {C2_HATCH}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{C2_FIND} flickers, and by it {HERO} reads {C2_SIGN} above {C2_HATCH}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she opens {C2_HATCH} and follows {C2_PATH} leading inward, warm and humming, toward the center of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both ways bring {HERO} to {ROUTE_C_DOOR} that opens on {ROTUNDA}, and {COMPANION} squeezes eagerly through'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='past the door the great rotunda opens under its {DEADLINE_SIGN}, the true heart of the clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{CLOCK} stands at its center, and {HERO} is certain {GOAL} must be near it'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the whale-rib catwalk sways as {HERO} climbs it, and out over the middle of the dark hall {C_GATE} swings loose with a clang, leaving a gap she would have to jump'>>

choices.label: Cling to the rope and back off.
Try to jump {C_GATE} in the dark.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='{HERO} backs off {C_GATE}, clinging to the guide rope, and edges her way carefully back to the solid start of the catwalk, her hands aching'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='safe at the foot of the catwalk again, {HERO} decides the high dark crossing is not worth it and wades in among the floor tanks instead'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='{HERO} tries to jump the gap in the dark, but she cannot see the far side and freezes on {C_GATE}, too scared to go on or back; she clings there safely above the soft tank-nets until, at opening time, a keeper hears {COMPANION} squeaking and helps her down, her night of exploring over'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='{ROUTE_D_ZONE} glows softly, clockwork butterflies and beetles resting on glass ferns beneath a domed ceiling painted with stars, everything still and glittering'>>

choices.label: Wander in among the glass ferns.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='{HERO} can wander through the low fern beds among the resting insects, or climb the iron mezzanine gallery that runs around the glass walls above'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='in the fern beds {HERO} can visit {D1_ZONE}, or {D2_ZONE} where little brass beetles crowd a keeper bench'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{D1_ZONE} is full of clockwork butterflies with wings of thin bright foil, folded and still, waiting for a wind that never came'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{COMPANION} noses {D1_FIND}, and {HERO} sees each butterfly has a keyhole no bigger than a seed, far too small for any ordinary key'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='beside {D1_FIND} {D1_CLUE} tells of one {GOAL} that turns the whole museum at once from {ROTUNDA}, and {HERO} memorises the way'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='she follows {D1_PATH}, {COMPANION} scurrying ahead between the glass ferns toward the middle of the museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{D1_PATH} ends at {D1_DOOR}, humming gently, {CLOCK} beating slow and deep beyond it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{D2_ZONE} is a busy little world of brass beetles gathered round a keeper bench scattered with tiny tools'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='in a half-open drawer {HERO} finds {D2_FIND} of the conservatory and the halls beyond, marked in a keeper hand'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{D2_FIND} shows a short way to {ROTUNDA}, and {D2_SIGN} seems to point along the route'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{HERO} follows {D2_PATH} between the glass cases to {D1_DOOR}, warm and humming, that leads toward the museum heart'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='both ways lead {HERO} to {ROUTE_D_DOOR} that opens on {ROTUNDA}, and {COMPANION} slips through the crack ahead of her'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the arch gives on the round rotunda under its {DEADLINE_SIGN}, the true center of the clockwork museum'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='{CLOCK} rises in the middle, and {HERO} knows {GOAL} must hang close by'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the mezzanine gallery is edged with old glass panels, and as {HERO} leans to look down, {D_GATE} shifts loose with a grinding sound and tips slowly inward'>>

choices.label: Step back from the loose glass.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='{HERO} snatches her hands back and steps away as {D_GATE} settles crookedly against the rail, then she retreats along the gallery to the safe stairs down'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='back on the conservatory floor, shaken, {HERO} decides the mezzanine is too fragile and explores the fern beds below instead'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='{HERO} edges past {D_GATE}, but another shifts behind her and a glass door swings shut across the gallery, closing her gently in {D_STUCK}; nothing breaks and she is quite safe, but she must wait, tapping to {COMPANION} through the glass, until the keepers open the gallery in the morning'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=128 beats='BOTTLENECK: every wing of the museum leads here to {ROTUNDA}, and {HERO} finds what she came for; high on a brass hook beside {CLOCK} hangs {GOAL}, the one key that can wind the whole menagerie at once; she climbs {KEY_LADDER} and lifts it down, warm and heavy, while {COMPANION} cheers in tiny squeaks; three ways lead on from the rotunda, through {K_A_ZONE}, along {K_B_ZONE}, or down {K_C_ZONE}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{K_A_ZONE} open on {KA_ROOM} where the whole museum clockwork lives, wheels and shafts reaching up into the dark'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} follows {KA_GANTRY}, {COMPANION} pattering ahead, toward {KA_DRUM} at the very center of it all'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{KA_GANTRY} ends above {KA_WORKS}, waiting cold and quiet'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{K_B_ZONE} is lined with paintings of old keepers, and their painted eyes seem to follow {HERO} toward a door at the far end'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the last portrait is {KB_PORTRAIT}, and beneath it a brass plate reads {CARETAKER}, tender of the works'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the door below his portrait opens on a walkway that looks down into {KA_ROOM}, the heart of the whole museum'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='{K_C_ZONE} are cramped and dark, and at the bottom {KC_GATE} bars the way; {HERO} can try to squeeze carefully through the gap beside it, or shove hard against the turnstile itself'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} breathes in and slips sideways through the narrow gap, {COMPANION} riding her collar, into {KC_PASSAGE} beyond {KC_GATE}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{KC_PASSAGE} climbs and opens onto a ledge above {KA_ROOM}, {KA_WORKS} spread out below'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=96 beats='{HERO} shoves {KC_GATE} with all her weight, but it swings the wrong way and latches shut behind her, penning her in the little dark stairwell with no way back up; she is safe and {COMPANION} keeps her company, but she can only sit and wait on the steps until the morning staff come and unlatch {KC_GATE} to let her out'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='BOTTLENECK: all three ways bring {HERO} out above {WORKROOM}, and here she learns why the whole menagerie fell asleep; {CRISIS} has snapped clean across, its broken ends sprung apart, and nothing can be wound until it is mended; even {GOAL} is useless against a broken heart of steel; {COMPANION} scratches worriedly at the floor, and {HERO} sees three ways to look for help, {S_A_ZONE}, {S_B_ZONE}, and {S_C_ZONE}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{S_A_ZONE} is a deep tin chest of spanners and oil, and on top lies {SA_FIND}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{COMPANION} burrows under the tools and turns up {SA_CLUE}, a keeper drawing of how a broken spring is clamped and changed'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{SA_CLUE} in hand, {HERO} follows a keeper sign from {S_A_ZONE} toward {S_DOOR}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{S_B_ZONE} is stacked with worn keeper books, their spines labelled winding, oiling, and mending the great spring'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} pulls down the mending book and its pages fall open to {SB_CLUE}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the book names {S_DOOR}, and {HERO} hurries to find it, {COMPANION} scampering at her heels'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='where {COMPANION} scratches, a loose floor panel hides a crawlspace, but {SC_GATE} leans over it, only half-braced; {HERO} can ease the panel up slowly and gently, or yank it free in a hurry'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} lifts the panel a careful inch at a time, keeping clear of {SC_GATE}, and uncovers {SC_PASSAGE} running behind the machine'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='she crawls through {SC_PASSAGE} with {COMPANION} and comes up beside {S_DOOR}, the very one the keeper must have used'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=96 beats='{HERO} yanks the panel free, but the sudden jerk unbalances {SC_GATE} and it topples with a great clang across {SC_PASSAGE}, blocking it and pinning the hem of her coat; she wriggles safely out of her coat, unhurt, but the way is buried now and she must wait by {CRISIS} until the keepers arrive at dawn to shift the gear'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='BOTTLENECK: each way leads to the same low door, and behind it {HERO} finds {WORKSHOP}, snug and warm and full of wonders; here are spare springs coiled on pegs, oil and clamps and the special tool for changing a mainspring, and on the bench {LETTER} explaining that he has been taken poorly and cannot come, with careful drawings of how to mend the great spring; now {HERO} has {GOAL}, {SPARE}, and the knowledge to use them; three things call to her, {P_A_ZONE}, {P_B_ZONE}, and {LETTER}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{P_A_ZONE} lies open at the very page {HERO} needs, each step of changing the great spring drawn in patient keeper ink'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} reads it twice until she is sure she understands, {COMPANION} following her finger down the page like a tiny student'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='confident now, she gathers the tool and {SPARE} and heads back toward {WORKROOM} to begin'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{SPARE} hangs coiled and gleaming on {P_B_ZONE}, as long as {HERO} is tall, heavier than it looks when she lifts it down'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='she loads it onto {PB_CART} with the changing tool, {COMPANION} perched on top to keep watch'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='wheeling {PB_CART} carefully, {HERO} makes her way back toward the broken heart of the museum'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='{LETTER} mentions {PC_GATE} that could speed the heavy spring back to {WORKROOM}; {HERO} can carry the spring the long safe way in her own arms, or throw {PC_GATE} to send {PB_CART} quickly'>>

choices.label: Carry the spring the long safe way.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} lifts the heavy spring in her own arms and carries it step by careful step, {COMPANION} cheering her on, back toward {WORKROOM}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='breathing hard, she sets the spring down beside the broken one, everything she needs now gathered in one place'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=96 beats='{HERO} throws {PC_GATE} and {PB_CART} jerks away too fast, rattling off down a side track with {SPARE} aboard and vanishing behind a locked shutter; nothing is broken and {HERO} is fine, but {SPARE} is out of reach now, and she can only wait by the machine until the keepers open the shutter in the morning'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='BOTTLENECK: every path ends here at {WORKROOM}, where {HERO} stands with {GOAL} in one hand, a shining {SPARE} at her feet, and {CARETAKER}'s instructions clear in her head; through the tall windows {DEADLINE_SIGN}, and soon the doors will open and children will pour in hoping to see the animals move; there is time for one brave choice; {HERO} can try to change the great spring and rewind the whole menagerie, mend just one beloved creature she has come to love, follow {CARETAKER}'s deepest secret, or simply keep the museum safe and let the quiet dawn come'>>

choices.label: Change the great spring and rewind them all.
Keep the museum safe until dawn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='changing {CRISIS} is the hardest task of all, and how {HERO} does it matters; she can work slow and careful by the manual, work bold and fast to beat the dawn, or try a clever trick from {CARETAKER}'s notes to make it easier'>>

choices.label: Work slow and careful by the manual.
Work bold and fast to beat the dawn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='step by careful step {HERO} clamps {CRISIS}, eases the ends apart, and fits {SPARE} exactly as the manual shows, {COMPANION} passing her each tool'>>

choices.label: Fit the master key to the shaft.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the new spring is in and {GOAL} fits the great winding shaft; {HERO} can wind it fully now so the whole menagerie wakes for the morning crowd, or wind it gently and set it to stir only when {CARETAKER} himself returns'>>

choices.label: Wind it fully and wake them all for dawn.
Set them to wake when Fettle returns.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='racing the light, {HERO} forces the spring in fast; it is nearly seated but not quite, and she must choose, to stop and reseat it properly or wind it now and hope'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} makes herself slow down, backs the spring out, and seats it properly this time, and the great works take {GOAL} with a deep satisfying click'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='following {CARETAKER}'s clever notes, {HERO} links {SPARE} through a helper gear that takes half the strain, and the mend goes smoother than she dared hope'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='with the trick done, {CRISIS} holds firm, and {GOAL} turns the whole museum with a low waking hum'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=100 beats='{HERO} winds {GOAL} and all through the museum the menagerie stirs to life at once, brass birds bursting into song, {ANIMAL_LION} stretching, {ANIMAL_WHALE} turning slow on its wires, the great elephant lifting its trunk in a gentle trumpet; when the doors open at dawn the children gasp with joy to see every animal moving, and {CARETAKER}, hearing the news, weeps with gladness that a stranger saved his whole life's work; the very best of endings'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='{HERO} sets {CRISIS} gently, so the animals will wake slow and easy the moment {CARETAKER} turns {GOAL} himself; she leaves everything mended and ready, and when {CARETAKER} comes home at last he finds his menagerie whole and waiting, and knows a kind hand has been here; a warm and thoughtful ending'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='the reseated spring holds, and {HERO} winds the museum awake; most of the menagerie stirs beautifully, and though a few small far-off animals sleep on, the morning crowd still marvels at a hall of moving wonders, and {HERO} grins to have very nearly done it all; a proud ending'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=94 beats='{HERO} winds the half-seated spring and it slips with a twang, springing loose so the mend must be done all over again; nothing is harmed, but there is no time now before the doors open, and the animals sleep on through the morning while {HERO} waits, a little sad, to explain it all to the keepers; a gentle setback'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='{HERO}'s clever mend holds strong, and {GOAL} wakes the menagerie smoothly, animal after animal coming alive down the halls; the keepers later shake their heads in wonder at the neat helper gear, and say the child who did this has the makings of a fine keeper; a clever, happy ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='{HERO} cannot mend the whole great spring in time, but with {GOAL} and a little spare wind she could wake just one animal, and she thinks of the ones she has met tonight; {ANIMAL_BIRD}, {ANIMAL_LION}, {ANIMAL_WHALE}, {ANIMAL_FLY}, or {ANIMAL_ELEPH}'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} hurries to {ROUTE_A_ZONE} and fits the little winder to {ANIMAL_BIRD}'s tiny keyhole, turning it with the greatest care'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{ANIMAL_BIRD} trembles, ruffles its brass feathers, and {COMPANION} watches in delight as the little bird gathers itself to sing'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='{ANIMAL_BIRD} bursts into the sweetest clockwork song, filling the whole aviary with silver notes, and when the doors open the children crowd to hear the one bird singing its heart out in the hush; {CARETAKER} says a museum with even one waking animal is a museum full of hope; a bright, joyful ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} crosses to {ROUTE_B_ZONE} and fits the key to {ANIMAL_LION}, winding until she feels its spring grow tight and ready'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{ANIMAL_LION} shivers, its tin mane shaking, and slowly lifts its heavy head as the last of the wind takes hold'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='{ANIMAL_LION} opens its jaws and gives a long rolling roar that echoes through every hall, and the sleeping museum seems to shiver with it; the morning children jump and then cheer, and {HERO} laughs, proud of her brave waking beast; a grand ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} returns to {ROUTE_C_ZONE} and reaches up to wind {ANIMAL_WHALE} where it hangs on its wires'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{ANIMAL_WHALE}'s flukes stir, its great copper body swinging slow, and a deep hum builds inside it like a song from far under the sea'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='{ANIMAL_WHALE} lets out a long, low, beautiful call that rolls through the blue hall, and turns gently on its wires as if swimming; when the children come they stand hushed beneath it, gazing up in wonder, and {HERO} feels she has given the museum its very heart back; a wondrous ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} slips into {ROUTE_D_ZONE} and fits the seed-small winder to {ANIMAL_FLY} with the lightest touch'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the foil wings quiver, unfold, and catch the {DEADLINE_SIGN} in a hundred colours as the little spring takes hold'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='{ANIMAL_FLY} lifts from its glass fern and flutters in slow bright circles beneath the starry dome, and one by one {HERO} winds a few more until a little cloud of colour drifts through the conservatory; the children who come will chase them with shining eyes; a delicate, magical ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{HERO} goes to {ANIMAL_ELEPH} that frightened her earlier and, no longer afraid, fits {GOAL} to wind it true'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{ANIMAL_ELEPH} steadies as the proper wind fills its spring, its lurching gone, its movements smooth and calm at last'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='{ANIMAL_ELEPH} that lurched so scarily in the dark now sways gently and lifts its trunk in a soft, friendly trumpet, tame and calm and no longer a runaway; the morning children ride giggling in a circle around it, and {HERO} is proudest of all to have made the frightening thing gentle; a heart-warming ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='{LETTER} hinted at a deeper secret hidden in the museum, and {HERO} burns to know it; she can read {SEC_JOURNAL} on the bench, follow where {SEC_MOUSE} keeps leading, or open {SEC_CABINET_ZONE} in the workshop'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{SEC_JOURNAL} is thick and worn, and {HERO} turns its pages by candle light, {COMPANION} curled warm against her arm'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='page by page she learns how {CARETAKER} built the whole menagerie himself, animal by animal, over a long and lonely lifetime of loving work'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the last entry tells of his dearest wish, that one day a child would come who cared enough to keep the animals alive after him'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='{HERO} closes {SEC_JOURNAL} understanding everything now, that the menagerie was one old man's life poured lovingly into brass and tin, and that his greatest hope was simply a child like her; she leaves him a note promising to come back, and when {CARETAKER} reads it he knows his life's work is safe; a tender, meaningful ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='{SEC_MOUSE} keeps darting ahead and looking back, plainly wanting {HERO} to follow; she can chase after it into the dark, or coax it gently to come to her instead'>>

choices.label: Chase the mouse into the dark.
Coax the mouse gently to you.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='{SEC_MOUSE} leads {HERO} to a tiny door behind the skirting where {SEC_MOUSE_NEST} sleeps'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='{SEC_MOUSE} leads {HERO} to {CARETAKER}'s secret, {SEC_MOUSE_NEST} he made for company, and she winds them one by one until a dozen tiny helpers scurry out to tend the sleeping museum; {CARETAKER} is overjoyed that someone found his small friends, and {COMPANION} now has a whole family; a sweet, surprising ending'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='{SEC_MOUSE} will not be caught and simply sits, whiskers twitching, keeping {HERO} company through the last of the night; she never learns quite where it wanted to lead her, but she is not alone, and dawn finds the two of them waiting quietly together for the keepers to arrive; a calm, gentle ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='{SEC_CABINET_ZONE} is old and beautiful, and {HERO} is sure it holds {CARETAKER}'s greatest secret; she can try {SEC_CABINET_KEYS} one by one, or force the little lock with a workshop tool'>>

choices.label: Force the lock with a tool.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='patiently {HERO} tries key after key until at last one turns with a soft click, and {SEC_CABINET_ZONE}'s door swings open'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='inside stands {SEC_RABBIT}, older than all the rest, with a note that it must always be kept safe'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=98 beats='{SEC_CABINET_ZONE} opens on {CARETAKER}'s first creation, {SEC_RABBIT}, the seed of the whole menagerie; {HERO} winds it with the greatest care and the old rabbit hops slowly, faithfully, as it must have a lifetime ago; {CARETAKER} can hardly believe his first friend lives again, and calls {HERO} the finest thing that ever happened to his museum; a precious, moving ending'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=94 beats='{HERO} forces the little lock with a tool, but the delicate old catch snaps and {SEC_CABINET_ZONE} jams tight shut, refusing to open at all; nothing is harmed that a keeper cannot mend, but she never sees the secret inside, and must wait rather sheepishly to tell {CARETAKER} she was too impatient; a small setback'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='perhaps the bravest thing is simply to keep the sleeping museum safe and let {DEADLINE_SIGN} come; but even so {HERO} has choices, she can let the animals sleep and wait, wind just one favourite before the light, or wait by the door to meet whoever comes'>>

choices.label: Let them sleep and wait for morning.
Wait by the door to meet someone.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='{HERO} settles on {WORKROOM}'s floor with {COMPANION} in her lap, watching the grey light climb the tall windows over the sleeping animals'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='{HERO} keeps the whole menagerie safe through the night and lets {DEADLINE_SIGN} come gently, the animals still and sleeping, everything exactly as the keeper left it; she did not fix the great spring, but nothing came to harm, and when the doors open she can tell the keepers all she learned; a safe, quiet, unfinished ending'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='{HERO} thinks of all the animals and picks the one that touched her most, hurrying to it with {GOAL} as the sky pales'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='she fits {GOAL} with care and gives it just enough wind to wake for the morning, saving the rest for the keepers'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the animal stirs to gentle life under her hands, and she steps back to watch, glad she chose it'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='{HERO} wakes one single favourite animal to greet {DEADLINE_SIGN} while the rest sleep on, safe and ready; when the children come they find one wonderful moving creature among the still ones, a promise of all the wonders to come once {CARETAKER} mends the rest, and {HERO} treasures the quiet magic of her one small waking; a lovely, hopeful ending'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='{HERO} waits by the great front door as the light grows, and she can watch for the museum keepers arriving, leave a helpful note pinned where they will see it, or tuck herself out of sight until she understands who is coming'>>

choices.label: Leave a helpful note for the morning.
Hide until you know who it is.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='{HERO} writes out everything on {DAWN_NOTE}, the broken spring, {SPARE}, the steps to mend it, and pins it where the morning staff cannot miss it'>>
<<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='she props {GOAL} beside it, ready for whoever comes, and waits nearby to explain if they need her'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=96 beats='{DAWN_NOTE} tells the morning keepers exactly how to mend the great spring, and with it they wake the whole menagerie by opening time; the children never know how close the museum came to a silent day, but {HERO} knows, and the grateful keepers say her clear thinking saved the morning; a clever, satisfying ending'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='{HERO} waits quietly and meets the first keeper through the door at dawn, and tells her the whole story of the broken spring and the long night; the keeper listens amazed, thanks her kindly, and sees her safely home, and though {HERO} did not mend the menagerie herself, she leaves knowing she helped; a calm, friendly ending'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=94 beats='{HERO} hides as the door opens, but it is only {DAWN_CLEANER}, who does not know a child is there and locks the inner gate on the way through, shutting {HERO} in the hall a while longer; she is safe and it is soon sorted out, but her chance to explain the broken spring slips by, and the menagerie sleeps through the morning; a gentle setback'>>
